title: MechanismArchetype
description: Schema for Mechanism Ascriptions — the logical causal unit. Defines the owning Structure, function label, and (in Generative mode) the Starlark rule. In Declarative mode, rule is absent and Effectors/Receptors are authored explicitly. DM input = DM output (1:1). Tenant-defined archetypes extending Mechanism MUST reference this schema via allOf.

structure.description: ID reference to the owning Structure.

function.description: Capability label describing what the Mechanism does (e.g., 'UserValidation', 'PaymentRouting', 'OrderTransformation'). MUST be unique within the owning Structure's scope — two Mechanisms in the same Structure cannot share the same function.

rule.description:
Starlark source code defining the Mechanism's behavioral logic. Present in Generative mode; absent in Declarative mode. Mutual exclusivity: when rule is present, Effectors/Receptors are auto-derived from the rule AST by the definition-manager; when rule is absent, Effectors/Receptors MUST be authored explicitly as separate Ascriptions.

## Sandboxed environment
The rule body executes in a sandboxed Starlark environment exposing exactly: the `sys` namespace, injected host functions (`on`, `now`, `uuid7`, `fullmatch`, `search`), and Starlark built-ins. No other globals are permitted. No `load()` statements allowed. Execution budget: configurable max steps (default 100k).

## sys namespace
- `sys.id` — read-only property: the Mechanism's `id`.

## Event trigger (Receptor derivation)
- `on("ArchetypeName")` — MUST be the first executable statement. Declares the triggering Archetype and returns its payload. Auto-derives a Receptor typed by the named Archetype.

## Output calls (Effector derivation)
- `sys.emit("ArchetypeName", data)` — emits data typed by the named Archetype. Auto-derives an Effector.
- `sys.emit("ArchetypeName", data, response="RespArchetype")` — emits with synchronous response expectation. Auto-derives an Effector (emit) + Receptor (response).
- `sys.create("ArchetypeName", data)` — creates a state object. Auto-derives an Effector.
- `sys.modify("ArchetypeName", filter, data)` — modifies state object(s). Auto-derives an Effector.
- `sys.delete("ArchetypeName", filter)` — deletes state object(s). Auto-derives an Effector.
- `sys.acquire("ArchetypeName", filter)` — reads/acquires state object(s). Auto-derives an Effector.

## Closed-loop vs open-loop
- `var = sys.create(...)` (assigned) → closed-loop: auto-derives a feedback Receptor for verification.
- `sys.create(...)` (unassigned) → open-loop / fire-and-forget: no feedback Receptor derived.
- Same pattern for `sys.modify`, `sys.delete`, `sys.acquire`.

## Injected host functions
- `on(archetype)` — event trigger (see above).
- `now()` → ISO 8601 timestamp.
- `uuid7()` → UUIDv7 string (RFC 9562, time-sortable).
- `fullmatch(pattern, string)` → boolean (full regex match).
- `search(pattern, string)` → first regex match or None.

## Constraints
- MUST begin with exactly one `on("...")` call as first executable statement.
- First argument (archetype name) in all `sys.*` calls MUST be a string literal (enables static analysis and Effector/Receptor auto-derivation).
- All archetype name literals MUST resolve to declared Archetypes in the governed scope.
